youtube-shorts.keyChanges: YouTube Shorts now prioritizes watch time and engagement over pure views, uses AI to auto-tag content for discovery, treats Shorts as a separate feed with its own recommendation engine, and rewards consistent, high‑quality uploads with stronger promotion.
youtube-shorts.editingTips: Shoot vertically 9:16, limit to 60 seconds, start with a hook, use captions and text overlays, keep pacing tight, include trending audio, add quick cuts, and finish with a clear CTA.
youtube-shorts.postingTips: Post 1–3 Shorts daily, aim for early morning or late evening slots, use analytics to find peak audience times, and consider Shorts premieres for hot topics.
youtube-shorts.titleTips: Keep titles under 10 characters, include a keyword, use emojis sparingly, avoid clickbait, and add "Short" to signal format.
youtube-shorts.descriptionTips: Start with the keyword, add 2–3 relevant hashtags, link to your channel, keep it concise, and end with a call‑to‑action.
summaries: Shorts prioritize watch time over views
AI tags drive discovery in Shorts

tiktok.keyChanges: Algorithm now prioritizes completion rate, AI‑driven content taxonomy, longer 10‑minute videos, cross‑platform signals, and creator authenticity metrics.
tiktok.editingTips: Use 9:16 vertical format, hook in first 3 seconds, add dynamic captions, keep cuts fast, use trending sounds, leverage AI editing tools for color grading and transitions, optimize audio levels, include a clear CTA overlay.
tiktok.postingTips: Post 1–3 times daily, vary times (early morning, lunch, evening), target peak traffic hours (6–9 am, 12–2 pm, 6–10 pm local time), use analytics to adjust, schedule consistent batches, and engage with comments within first hour.
tiktok.titleTips: Keep titles under 5 words, add emojis, include a hook phrase, use relevant hashtags, add a subtle CTA, keep capitalization minimal.
tiktok.descriptionTips: Limit to 150 characters, embed 2–3 primary hashtags, add a call to action, include a link if eligible, use location tags, and keep the tone conversational.

instagram.keyChanges: Reels dominate feed, AI predicts relevance, emphasis on authenticity, content quality score, deeper integration of Stories.
instagram.editingTips: Use vertical 9:16, 4K resolution, add captions, keep first 3 seconds hook, color grade, use carousel for depth.
instagram.postingTips: Post 4-6 times weekly, peak 9‑11 AM EST, Reels daily, respond within first hour, schedule globally.
instagram.titleTips: Hook in 30 chars, use emoji, brand voice, keyword, keep concise.
instagram.descriptionTips: Storytelling first, clear CTA, 5‑10 hashtags, line breaks, use emojis sparingly, location tag.

youtube-long.keyChanges: Algorithm now prioritizes watch time, engagement, and AI‑generated content summaries; filler content is penalized, captions and structured metadata are essential, and 'Long' videos are treated as a distinct recommendation queue.
youtube-long.editingTips: Start with a 15‑second hook, use clear chapters, add auto‑captions, keep pacing tight with smooth transitions, optimize audio quality, and include a strong call‑to‑action at the end.
youtube-long.postingTips: Post 2–3 times weekly, ideally on Tuesdays and Thursdays between 10 am–12 pm UTC, maintain consistent series structure, and schedule uploads for peak audience hours using analytics.
youtube-long.titleTips: Keep titles under 70 characters, embed primary keyword, use a clear value proposition or question, avoid clickbait, and add a compelling hook within the first 4 words.
youtube-long.descriptionTips: Start with a concise summary, insert timestamps and chapter links, add relevant keywords naturally, include a CTA and social links, and use 2–3 hashtags for discoverability.

facebook-reels.keyChanges: Algorithm now prioritizes watch time, contextual relevance, and AI‑driven personalization across Reels, with a stronger focus on short, engaging clips and cross‑platform visibility.
facebook-reels.editingTips: Use high‑quality footage, clear storytelling, dynamic cuts, captions, trending audio, keep clips under 15‑20 seconds, add visible call‑to‑action, optimize for vertical mobile viewing, and incorporate brand elements throughout.
facebook-reels.postingTips: Post during early morning or early evening local time, maintain a consistent schedule of 3–4 times per week, monitor audience activity windows, and experiment with pause‑and‑watch metrics to refine timing.
facebook-reels.titleTips: Keep titles concise (≤10 words), include a keyword or hook, use emojis for personality, add brand name, and end with a call‑to‑action if relevant.
facebook-reels.descriptionTips: Include 3–5 relevant hashtags, mention location or partner, add a short CTA, keep description under 200 characters, and link to a landing page or profile when possible.
